articles.title: Congress protests water crisis in Jaipur amid heatwave - Awaz The Voice
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Rajasthan
articles.full_text:
Jaipur (Rajasthan)
Congress workers on Monday staged a protest at Jal Bhavan in Jaipur over an acute water shortage amid severe heatwave conditions, alleging that the state government has failed to ensure adequate drinking water supply to the public.
The protest was led by former minister and Congress leader Pratap Singh Khachariyawas.
Speaking to ANI during the protest, Khachariyawas launched a sharp attack on the state government, calling it "incompetent" over its handling of the situation.
"Any government that cannot provide drinking water is an incompetent government. They failed to formulate a contingency plan," he said.
He further warned that if the Jaipur water crisis is not resolved within 24 hours and supply from Bisalpur is not increased, the water connections of the Chief Minister and ministers would be disconnected.
"If, within 24 hours, the issue of contaminated water in Jaipur is not resolved and if the supply from Bisalpur is not increased to make water available to the people, we will cut off the water connections of the Chief Minister and his ministers," Khachariyawas said.
He also alleged that the government is not listening to public concerns, adding that the protest is currently focused on Jal Bhawan and could later extend to the Chief Minister's residence.
"There is no one in this government willing to listen to the public. The people made a grave mistake by casting so many votes in their favour. Today, we are laying siege to the 'Jal Bhawan'; our next destination will be to lay siege to the Chief Minister's official residence," Khachariyawas added.
The protest comes as several parts of the country continue to reel under intense heatwave conditions, with Rajasthan among the worst-affected regions.
From Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar and Rajasthan to Maharashtra, Telangana and Gujarat, citizens are facing scorching temperatures, water shortages and hot winds, while health authorities have urged people to stay indoors during peak afternoon hours and maintain hydration.
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), heatwave conditions are expected to persist for the next several days across large parts of the country.
Heatwave conditions have also impacted tourism in Rajasthan's Udaipur, with local businesses reporting a sharp decline in tourist arrivals.
Earlier, Jaipur Meteorological Centre Director Radheshyam Sharma said dry weather conditions are likely to continue across most parts of Rajasthan, with heatwave conditions expected to persist for the coming days despite a slight temporary dip in temperature.

articles.title: Congress protests water shortage in Jaipur amid heatwave, Khachariyawas issues 24-hour ultimatum - The News Mill
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Rajasthan
articles.full_text:
Congress workers gathered at Jal Bhavan in Jaipur on Monday to protest an acute water shortage during severe heatwave conditions, accusing the Rajasthan state government of failing to provide adequate drinking water. The demonstration was led by former minister and Congress leader Pratap Singh Khachariyawas.
Addressing the protest, Khachariyawas sharply criticised the state government as “incompetent” for its management of the crisis. “Any government that cannot provide drinking water is an incompetent government. They failed to formulate a contingency plan,” he stated.
Khachariyawas issued a 24-hour ultimatum to the government to resolve the Jaipur water crisis and increase supply from Bisalpur. He warned that, should these demands not be met, the water connections of the Chief Minister and ministers would be disconnected. “If, within 24 hours, the issue of contaminated water in Jaipur is not resolved and if the supply from Bisalpur is not increased to make water available to the people, we will cut off the water connections of the Chief Minister and his ministers,” he said.
He also accused the government of ignoring public concerns and indicated that the protest, currently focused on Jal Bhawan, might extend to the Chief Minister’s residence. “There is no one in this government willing to listen to the public. The people made a grave mistake by casting so many votes in their favour. Today, we are laying siege to the ‘Jal Bhawan’; our next destination will be to lay siege to the Chief Minister’s official residence,” Khachariyawas added.
The protest occurred amid ongoing heatwave conditions affecting several parts of India, with Rajasthan among the worst-hit areas. Citizens in Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Rajasthan, Maharashtra, Telangana, and Gujarat face extreme temperatures, water shortages, and hot winds. Health authorities have advised staying indoors during peak afternoon hours and maintaining hydration.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) anticipates that heatwave conditions will persist across much of the country for the next several days. In Rajasthan, the heatwave has also adversely affected tourism in Udaipur, with local businesses noting a sharp decline in visitor numbers.
Radheshyam Sharma, Director of the Jaipur Meteorological Centre, reported that dry weather is expected to continue across most of Rajasthan and that heatwave conditions will likely persist despite a slight temporary temperature decrease.

articles.title: Rajasthan Reels Under Intense Heatwave as Temperatures Soar Toward 47°C - The Morning Voice
articles.full_text:
Rajasthan Reels Under Intense Heatwave as Temperatures Soar Toward 47°C
Rajasthan is in the grip of a severe heatwave , with temperatures rising sharply across several parts of the state and conditions expected to intensify further in the coming days.
In western Rajasthan, daytime temperatures are consistently hovering between 45°C and 46°C , making conditions extremely harsh during peak afternoon hours. The situation has been particularly severe in Jaisalmer , which recorded a maximum of 45.9°C in the past 24 hours, according to the Meteorological Department .
The Meteorological Centre, Jaipur has issued a heatwave alert, warning that dry weather is likely to persist across most parts of the state for the next three to four days. Officials also cautioned that dust laden winds with speeds of 20 to 30 kmph may continue, adding to the discomfort and reducing visibility in some regions.
Weather experts have indicated that temperatures could rise further by 2°C to 3°C , pushing the mercury even higher across western districts. The most severe phase is expected on May 26 and 27 , when parts of western Rajasthan may experience maximum temperatures reaching 46°C to 47°C , with isolated areas likely to face severe heatwave conditions .
Relief, however, is expected later in the month. A fresh western disturbance is likely to affect the region around May 28 and 29 , bringing possibilities of thunderstorms , strong winds reaching 50 to 60 kmph, and light rainfall in some areas. This change in weather may help bring down temperatures by 2°C to 3°C .
Officials added that intermittent thunderstorm and rainfall activity could continue into the first week of June, offering temporary relief from the ongoing intense heat affecting large parts of the state.
